summary: Matched part of Bowser/Koopa fighter code by splitting ftkoopa into a new C translation unit, adding ftKoopa headers and needed Fighter struct fields, and wiring the new object into the build. The PR removed about 540 lines from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.s and added src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.c with a mix of inline asm carryover and C implementations for simpler helpers/state routines. Body evidence is minimal beyond "Tested OK"; there were no review comments.
what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.c and moved a block of ftKoopa symbols out of the standalone asm file.
Kept many hard-to-decompile routines as inline `asm void` functions inside ftkoopa.c using `nofralloc` and `#pragma peephole on`, while converting simpler routines to normal C.
Implemented C helpers such as func_80132D38 and func_80132D7C as two calls to func_800704F0 using float constants lbl_804D9AC8 and lbl_804D9ACC.

Implemented float accessors func_80132DC0, func_80132DD0, func_80132DE0, func_80132DF0, func_80132E00, func_80132E10, and func_80132E20 by reading fields from `ft->x2D4_specialAttributes` cast to `ftKoopaAttributes`.

Implemented action/state setup routines func_80132EFC, func_80132F94, and func_8013302C in C, touching Fighter state variables, throw flags, action IDs 0x15B/0x161/348/349, and callbacks func_8013302C/func_801330E4.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftkoopa.h with `ftKoopaAttributes` and prototypes for func_80132EFC and func_8013302C.
Extended `Fighter` layout in src/melee/ft/fighter.h with x2200_ftcmd_var0 through x220C_ftcmd_var3, x2210_ThrowFlags, x2222_flag, and x2340_stateVar1 through x2350_stateVar5.
Added `ThrowFlags`, a bitfield/flags union used for x2210_ThrowFlags.
Promoted prototypes for func_8006EBA4 and func_8007E2D0 in include/functions.h.
Added ftkoopa.o entries to e_files.mk and obj_files.mk so the new C object participates in the build while the remaining asm ftkoopa object stays present.
smart_moves: Used a hybrid translation-unit approach: decompiled the tractable ftKoopa routines to C while preserving exact assembly for unresolved functions, allowing incremental matching without requiring the whole file to be understood at once.

Introduced a character-specific `ftKoopaAttributes` struct only as far as needed, using named float fields at offsets 0x34 through 0x4C and a byte gap for unknown middle data instead of overclaiming unknown semantics.
Expanded `Fighter` with offset-anchored fields where the new C code needed symbolic access, avoiding raw pointer arithmetic in the matched functions.
Kept function pointer references alive by renaming address loads in the remaining asm to C symbol names such as func_80132B38, func_8013302C, and func_801330E4.
Added missing shared function prototypes only for functions used by the new C code, limiting header churn.
Recorded that the PR was tested with the short body note "Tested OK".
decomp_lessons: For large fighter character files, splitting off a new C object while retaining the old asm object can be a practical intermediate step when only a subset of routines is matchable.
Simple getters from special attributes are good early matching targets because they reveal structure offsets and reduce future raw-offset usage.
State-variable fields near 0x2340 and ftcmd variables near 0x2200 are shared enough to belong in `Fighter`, not only in character-local code, when multiple routines need typed access.
Bitfield unions like `ThrowFlags` can match byte/word flag operations while still allowing C code to use semantic bit access such as `ft->x2222_flag.bits.b2 = 1`.
When converting action setup routines, preserve action IDs, callback pointers, and state-var zeroing order carefully; these details are visible in the diff and likely matching-sensitive.
If no review discussion exists, future agents should rely on the diff and PR body only; reviewer intent is not evidenced here.
naming_conventions: `ftKoopa_OnDeath` and `ftKoopa_OnLoad` follow the character lifecycle naming style for known callbacks.
Unknown ftKoopa functions remained address-based, e.g. `func_80132A64`, `func_80132EFC`, and `func_8013302C`.
Character-specific attributes were named `ftKoopaAttributes` with unknown fields as `unk34`, `unk38`, `unk3C`, `unk40`, `unk44`, `unk48`, and `unk4C`.
Fighter fields added in this PR use offset-prefixed names such as `x2200_ftcmd_var0`, `x2210_ThrowFlags`, and `x2340_stateVar1`.
Unknown flag structs and bitfields use generic names like `ThrowFlags`, `UnkFlagStruct`, and `bits.b2` where exact semantics are not known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline MetroWerks-style `asm void` functions with `nofralloc` to keep exact instruction sequences in a C file.
Applied `#pragma peephole on` after inline asm functions, a tactic commonly used in this project to control compiler output around hand-written asm blocks.
Translated short instruction sequences into direct C calls where matching was straightforward, e.g. two calls to func_800704F0 with constant floats.
Modeled repeated special-attribute float loads as typed C access through `((ftKoopaAttributes*) ft->x2D4_specialAttributes)->field`.
Preserved remaining assembly labels and function-pointer targets by adding small symbol references in ftkoopa.s to C-side symbols.
Used explicit function prototypes in include/functions.h for cross-module calls needed by the new C routines.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or submitted reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human body text was "Tested OK".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename ftKoopa address-based functions to semantic Bowser move names.
Search for subsequent changes to `ftKoopaAttributes` to replace `unk34` through `unk4C` with meaningful field names.
Check later Fighter struct layout revisions around offsets 0x2200, 0x2210, 0x2222, and 0x2340 to see whether these generic state vars were refined.
Look for PRs that fully convert inline asm functions from this ftkoopa.c split into normal C.
